items.title: Trump is set to deliver an election-focused national address tonight. Here's what to know
items.description: "Without free and fair elections, you don't have a country," Trump said this week when asked to preview the speech.

items.title: Trump suspends teleprompter operator over Kalshi bets allegations, White House says
items.description: Kalshi retained most of the profits, which were more than $90,000, from the trades made on public statements by President Trump.

items.title: SoftBank sinks 8% as Japanese chip stocks track Wall Street AI sell-off
items.description: Japanese AI-linked stocks tumbled as a fresh rout in U.S. semiconductor shares spread across Asia after Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing's outlook failed to reassure investors.

items.title: Elon Musk's Memphis AI empire is the epicenter of the data center backlash
items.description: Data center-related policy proposals, protests and litigation are underway across the country citing Colossus and Memphis as a cautionary tale.

items.title: SpaceX aborts Starship test flight, sending stock lower
items.description: Elon Musk said it would make another attempt "hopefully in a few days."  SpaceX completed the maiden test flight of its Starship V3 rocket in May.

items.title: Microsoft's Nadella criticizes Anthropic's Fable for being 'editorially controlled'
items.description: Microsoft enjoys close ties to Anthropic, but it's also working to help companies refine models that keep their data private.

items.title: Banking Committee's Sen. Rounds liked Warsh's 'tone' in first testimony as Fed chairman
items.description: Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh was on Capitol Hill for two days of testimony this week.

items.title: Netflix stock falls as earnings forecast disappoints, company says it will give fewer engagement updates
items.description: The streaming giant said it would cut back on the frequency of its "What We Watched" reports, which provide a picture of engagement.

items.title: AG pick Blanche to meet with Epstein survivors after 2 GOP senators hedge on nomination
items.description: Sen. Thom Tillis, R-N.C., wants Todd Blanche to meet with victims of sex offender Jeffrey Epstein before committing his support for the nomination.

items.title: Here's why the housing market is hurting so much this summer
items.description: Mortgage rates are high, home prices at record levels, and consumers stressed — all contributing to a drop in existing home sales and builder sentiment.

items.title: The best energy stocks right now as two major conflicts keep oil prices elevated
items.description: What Brian Sullivan is hearing from energy insiders.

items.title: Oil pipelines around the Strait of Hormuz won't end the threat Iran poses to Middle East crude exports
items.description: Oil producers are building or contemplating new pipeline projects to bypass the Strait of Hormuz, but this infrastructure is still vulnerable, analysts said.

items.title: Alphabet shares fall on report its most powerful AI model Gemini 3.5 Pro is delayed
items.description: Alphabet announced the Gemini 3.5 Pro AI  in May, saying it was being used internally but wouldn't be ready for a broader rollout until the following month.

items.title: UnitedHealth blows past estimates, hikes earnings outlook as it reins in costs
items.description: The healthcare giant is working to stabilize margins by shrinking membership, exiting unprofitable contracts and pouring $1.5 billion into AI.

items.title: Dallas Fed President Logan calls for 'modestly' higher interest rates
items.description: The policymaker said this week's good inflation news wasn't good enough.

items.title: Here's a rapid-fire update on our stock portfolio, including the 5 names to buy
items.description: Jim Cramer and Jeff Marks offered their latest thoughts on the Investing Club's portfolio during the July Monthly Meeting.

items.title: Trump Media launches paid data service to help Wall Street track Trump’s posts
items.description: Trump Media launched an API to give paying customers real-time access to market-moving Truth Social posts.

items.title: Short sellers load up against SpaceX as stock drops below IPO price
items.description: About 185 million SpaceX shares are now sold short, representing roughly 29% of the company's publicly tradable float, according to S3.

items.title: 'Not a junk rally:' How to trade the strongest small-cap stock market in three decades
items.description: The Russell 2000 small-cap stock index is up almost 20% this year, its best first half since 1991, and investing experts say the rally can keep running.

items.title: As prediction markets boom, questions arise over who will be the watchdog
items.description: As new contract types are introduced, prediction markets may have to work under two regulatory bodies.
